records.ety: [LL. situatus, from situare to place, fr. L. situs situation, site. See Site.]

defs: Having a site, situation, or location; being in a relative position; permanently fixed; placed; located; as, a town situated, or situate, on a hill or on the seashore.

quotes: Pleasure situate in hill and dale.
notes: ☞ Situate is now less used than situated, but both are well authorized.
